# Create the release-notes authoring agent skill

## Context

Leaf of epic #14483 — the operator-directed centerpiece (2026-07-02): "we must create a new agent skill this time. it requires heavy memory mining. it must meet the quality bar of previous release notes." No release-notes skill exists; the nearest siblings (`blog-post`, `guide-authoring`, `update-roadmap`) cover adjacent surfaces but not this protocol. v13.0's process reached the bar through an unformalized 7-leaf iteration cluster (#12695→#12924) and was lost.

Sweep: epic-reshape under live operator direction; no competing skill ticket (skills tree checked this session; latest-open sweep 14:42Z).

## The Problem

Every release re-derives the notes process from scratch, and single-pass derivation measurably fails: the v13.1 iteration-1 draft carried a War-Story environment error (LOCAL incident narrated under cloud framing), one-lens narration, and release-downplaying scope framing — precisely the misses a codified protocol with per-claim V-B-A prevents.

## The Fix

Author `.agents/skills/release-notes/` under `create-skill` + `turn-memory-pre-flight` discipline (Progressive Disclosure: thin SKILL.md router + workflow payload). The payload codifies:

1. **Scope derivation** — `analyzeClosedSinceRelease.mjs` (cutoff = previous release commit) + release project-board views + epic closures + the explicit caveat that the ticket record LAGS shipped reality (v13.1: est. 300–500 unfiled changes); no single tracker view is the window's truth.
2. **Heavy Memory-Core mining per narrative arc** — `query_summaries`/`query_raw_memories` sweeps over the window per candidate hero chapter; sessions and summaries ground the story, ticket titles do not.
3. **Per-claim V-B-A** — every factual claim carries a source anchor; environment claims (local vs cloud), scope claims (what a release "is"), and stability claims verified against primary sources before assertion.
4. **The quality-bar contract** distilled from the v13.0.0 notes (`resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v13.0.0.md`): narrative hero chapters, the "in 2 minutes" section, TL;DR discipline, honest bounds, upgrade-path framing, War-Story arc (Symptom → Investigation → Culprit → Fix) — and the operator directives as named rules: **do NOT downplay a release**, **multiple iterations ARE needed** (draft merges early with an explicit iteration marker; mining-driven refinement leaves follow until cut-readiness).
5. **Publish-flow mechanics** — flat-root staging lifecycle (`publish.mjs:83-226`: required pre-flight → atomic-hash append → GitHub Release parse (frontmatter/H1 contract) → self-removal post-release → chunk-N re-materialization), the orphan-guard staging window (#14484-refined), and the human-only cut gate.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.agents/skills/release-notes/SKILL.md` (thin router, trigger description) + `references/` workflow payload exist and pass the create-skill shape bar
- [ ] The five protocol areas above are each covered with concrete tool commands / file anchors, not prose gestures
- [ ] Substrate-mutation slot-rationale included in the PR body per turn-memory-pre-flight (new skill-loaded substrate: disposition + trigger-frequency rationale)
- [ ] The skill's quality-bar section cites the v13.0.0 notes as the measurable precedent and encodes the iteration cadence
- [ ] PR `Resolves` this leaf, `Refs` #14483

## Out of Scope

- Authoring the v13.1 iterations themselves (sibling leaves under #14483).
- Modifying `blog-post` / `guide-authoring` / `update-roadmap` (cross-reference only).

## Related

Related: #14483 (parent epic), #12695 (v13.0 lineage), #10321 (release-cut process sibling)

| `.agents/skills/skills.manifest.json` | manifest contract (ADR 0008) | New entry mirroring frontmatter + default budgets + claudeSymlinkRequired + both standard downstreamDocsTargets | Manifest drift → lint FAIL blocks merge | Manifest is self-documenting | `lint-skill-manifest --base origin/dev`: only the net-growth gate remains, discharged via `[skill-growth-justified]` commit tag (new-skill exception per the guide) |
